meta.notes: Exit-load explainer is backed by an official SEBI investor education page plus scheme-specific sources for exact exit-load rule.

scenarios.title: Exit load charged on redemption
scenarios.applies_to: Mutual fund redemptions where an exit load is applicable per scheme rules.
bullets: What it is: Exit load is a fee charged by the scheme if units are redeemed within the specified exit-load period. (Scheme rule: {exit_load_rule})
Why you may see it: If your redemption happened within that period (based on purchase date vs redemption date), the exit load can be deducted from the redemption proceeds.
Common triggers: Early redemption, partial redemptions of recent units (FIFO rules may apply), or switching between schemes within the exit-load window (depending on scheme terms).
What to verify (no PII): Check the transaction date(s) and redemption date; confirm which units were redeemed and whether they fall inside the exit-load period.
What this assistant can’t confirm: I can’t access your account/folio or transaction history. Please don’t share personal details here.

Next step: If you think it was applied incorrectly, compare your transaction dates against the scheme’s exit-load clause and contact support with your booking code (shared securely outside this chat).

scenarios.title: Expense ratio shown / applied in NAV (TER)
scenarios.applies_to: Questions about why an expense ratio is shown, or why it affects returns (it is accounted for in NAV).
bullets: What it is: The expense ratio (TER) is the scheme’s allowed operating expenses expressed as a % of daily net assets. (Shown TER: {expense_ratio})
Why you may feel 'charged': TER is typically adjusted in the scheme’s NAV on an ongoing basis; you usually don’t pay it as a separate bill.
What it covers (high level): Costs like investment management, administration, registrar/custody, and permitted marketing/distribution expenses (within regulations).
What to verify (facts-only): Check the scheme’s TER/expense ratio disclosure on official pages; TER can change over time as disclosed.
What this assistant can’t confirm: I can’t see your exact NAV history or account statements here. Please don’t share personal details.
Next step: If you want a human explanation of how TER impacts NAV for your holding period, book an advisor call (no PII in chat).

scenarios.title: Minimum SIP shown (not a separate fee)
scenarios.applies_to: Questions about why a minimum SIP amount is shown or why a SIP setup may be blocked below that amount.
bullets: What it is: Minimum SIP is the lowest allowed installment amount for starting a SIP in this scheme. (Minimum SIP shown: {min_sip})
Why you may think you were 'charged': Minimum SIP is not a fee; it’s a scheme constraint. It matters when you try to start/modify a SIP.
When it shows up: During SIP setup, when editing an existing SIP amount, or when validating an order before placement.
What to verify (facts-only): Confirm the scheme name and the SIP amount you entered; if it’s below the minimum, the app will prompt this value.
What this assistant can’t confirm: I can’t see your bank mandate, payments, or order history here. Please don’t share personal details.
Next step: If you believe money was deducted as a charge, check the transaction description in your statement and book an advisor call for support triage.
